channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Grid operator says Texas is better prepared for extreme cold this winter
item.description: ERCOT says conditions this winter could spark extreme cold, but additional power supply and continued weatherization efforts will lower the risk of power outages.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Kayla Guo, KPRC 2

item.title: DNC chair frontrunner offers 'uncomfortable' advice to Democrats after crushing loss to Trump
item.description: The candidate considered the frontrunner in the race to chair the DNC is proposing a "massive narrative and branding project" in the wake of November's election setbacks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Joe Biden's pardon of his son Hunter spurs broader discussion on who else should be granted clemency
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden's decision to break his word and pardon his son Hunter has spurred a broader discussion about what else he should be doing with the broad clemency powers of the presidency before he leaves office in January, including whether he should even be pardoning Donald T
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG, WILL WEISSERT and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democrats stick with Schumer as leader. Their strategy for countering Trump is far less certain
item.description: Senate Democrats reelected Chuck Schumer as party leader on Tuesday as the party moves into a deeply uncertain time, with no real consensus on a strategy as President-elect Donald Trump prepares to take office.
item.dc:creator: Mary Clare Jalonick, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: DNC chair candidate: Democrats must be willing to 'recenter' agenda
item.description: Ken Martin, chair of the Minnesota Democratic-Farm-Labor Party (DFL) and a candidate for the Democratic National Committee (DNC) chair, argued the party must be willing to “recenter” its agenda after it suffered a string of defeats during last month’s elections.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, NewsNation

item.title: The Associated Press' top photos of the 2024 US election
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — It's a photograph that defined the 2024 campaign. Donald Trump, campaigning for a second term, raises a fist defiantly toward the sky, blood streaked across his face. But the photo captures just one millisecond of a U.S. presidential campaign unlike any that came before it.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: McConnell cries foul after 2 Democratic judges cancel retirement after Trump victory
item.description: Senate Minority Leader Mitch McConnell (R-Ky.) vented his displeasure Monday after two Democratic-appointed federal judges reversed their decisions to retire in what appear to be efforts to stop President-elect Trump from nominating their successors.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jill Biden's final foreign trip as first lady will close with her and Trump at Notre Dame cathedral
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Jill Biden was departing Tuesday on her final solo foreign trip as first lady, a six-day, four-country jaunt through Italy, the United Arab Emirates and Qatar that wraps with her and President-elect Donald Trump joining other dignitaries in Paris to celebrate the reopening of Notre
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's FBI pick has plans to reshape the bureau. This is what Kash Patel has said he wants to do
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Kash Patel has been well-known for years within Donald Trump's orbit as a loyal supporter who shares the president-elect's skepticism of the FBI and intelligence community.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump vows to block Japanese steelmaker from buying US Steel, pledges tax incentives and tariffs
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is underscoring his intention to block the purchase of U.S. Steel by Japanese steelmaker Nippon Steel Corp., and he’s pledging to use tax incentives and tariffs to strengthen the iconic American steelmaker.
item.dc:creator: Marc Levy And Josh Boak, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Recently ousted Dem senator said he's 'getting the f--- out of here' when asked about presidential pardon
item.description: Recently ousted Sen. Jon Tester delivered an eyebrow raising response to reporters when asked about the president's controversial pardon of his son, Hunter Biden.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge closes Hunter Biden gun case after presidential pardon
item.description: Hunter Biden’s federal gun case was brought to a close by a judge Tuesday following the presidential pardon by his father. In a terse order, U.S. District Judge Maryellen Noreika terminated all proceedings in the case, citing President Biden’s executive grant of clemency to his son.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mar-a-Lago trumps White House as president-elect overshadows Biden on world stage
item.description: Has the Oval Office been replaced by Mar-a-Lago? That is the question being asked as world leaders increasingly look to President-elect Donald Trump rather than President Biden when it comes to diplomacy.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dem lawmaker joins Republican-led DOGE Caucus with push to remove Secret Service from DHS
item.description: Rep. Jared Moskowitz is calling for radical changes to the Department of Homeland Security in his announcement that he is joining the DOGE Caucus.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senate Democrats name top leadership positions after losing chamber majority
item.description: Democratic senators held an election on Tuesday to name who will assume their party's leadership roles for the 119th Congress.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: GOP lawmaker gifts 'chip of the Berlin Wall' to colleagues in bid for top committee spot
item.description: A House Republican running for the top spot on the Foreign Affairs Committee is giving pieces of the Berlin Wall to fellow lawmakers to boost his bid.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: At Supreme Court, vaping may be frowned upon, but that could change with Trump
item.description: The focal point of the case is 2009 law enacted by Congress that gives the Food and Drug Administration a mandate to curb the availability of nicotine products for minors.
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg, MPR News

item.title: Minnesota Republicans sue to force election rerun in tight House race where 20 ballots are missing
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — Minnesota Republicans filed a lawsuit Monday to try to force a rerun of a state House race where the incumbent Democrat won by 14 votes — but in which investigators concluded that election workers probably destroyed 20 valid absentee ballots after failing to count them.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democrat Jamie Raskin is running for top Judiciary post in bid to counter Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Rep. Jamie Raskin of Maryland announced Monday that he will run to become the top Democrat on the powerful House Judiciary Committee next year, directly challenging fellow Democratic Rep.
item.dc:creator: FARNOUSH AMIRI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US will send Ukraine $725 million more in counter-drone systems, anti-personnel land mines
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. is preparing to send Ukraine an additional $725 million in military assistance, including counter-drone systems and munitions for its High Mobility Artillery Rocket System, which could indicate more of the longer-range missiles are headed to the battlefield.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP and MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White House says Trump wouldn't 'let go' of Hunter Biden in defending pardon
item.description: The White House on Monday defended President Biden’s decision to pardon his son Hunter Biden after insisting for more than a year that he wouldn’t do so.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Trump picks longtime ally Kash Patel to replace current FBI director
item.description: The president-elect plans to replace current FBI Director Christopher Wray with Patel, a close ally of the president-elect and former national security aide, has berated the Justice Department and the news media.
item.dc:creator: Carrie Johnson, MPR News

item.title: Trump tariffs threaten to crack open North American economies
item.description: MEXICO CITY — President-elect Trump’s pledge to impose tariffs on Mexico and Canada threatens to upend decades of North American integration, a bumpy process that’s recast the two U.S. neighbors as providers of raw and finished goods for the world’s biggest economy.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Canada's Trudeau says he had an 'excellent conversation' with Trump after tariffs threat
item.description: The Canadian prime minister's meeting with Donald Trump at Mar-a-Lago came after the president-elect's threat to impose tariffs on two of America's leading trade partners raised alarms.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Democratic lawmakers report Thanksgiving bomb threats against their homes
item.description: At least five Democratic members of Congress from Connecticut were targeted by bomb threats on Thursday. Police who responded said they found no evidence of explosives on the lawmakers' properties.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump transition says Cabinet picks, appointees were targeted by bomb threats, swatting attacks
item.description: Among those targeted were New York Rep. Elise Stefanik, Trump's pick to serve as ambassador to the United Nations, and Matt Gaetz, Trump's initial pick to serve as attorney general.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump nominees targeted in series of threats, spokesperson says
item.description: (The Hill) - Multiple nominees and appointees tapped to serve in President-elect Trump’s incoming administration were targeted with threats this week, a spokesperson for the transition said Wednesday, though it was unclear how many were affected.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5
